jokes.text: Never go to a planet called 'Cannibalon.' That's right. Food was good, though.
jokes.setup: Warning about a dangerous planet called Cannibalon suggests they barely escaped with their lives
jokes.punchline: The casual admission that the food was good implies they were eating well-prepared people
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect misdirection - sets up a horror scenario then casually subverts it with cannibalistic implications

jokes.text: Great news, everyone- / Shove it! We quit! / Then I'll have to hire a new crew to go on our company vacation.
jokes.setup: Professor's typical 'great news' opening suggests another dangerous mission, crew quits preemptively
jokes.punchline: The 'great news' was actually a vacation, making their quit perfectly timed to miss out
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection that plays on established character patterns - Professor's announcements are usually terrible news

jokes.text: Great! I haven't had time off since I was 21 through 24.
jokes.setup: Fry expressing excitement about vacation time
jokes.punchline: The implication that he had a continuous 3-year vacation in his early twenties
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's slacker past, reasonably well-crafted

jokes.text: It's my way of thanking you for not reporting my countless violations... of safety and minimum-wage laws. / Aw, you!
jokes.setup: Professor being generous with a vacation
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's essentially hush money for illegal business practices, met with casual acceptance
jokes.explanation: Solid character humor showing Professor's casual criminality and crew's acceptance

jokes.text: I've booked us on the maiden voyage of the largest... most luxurious space cruise ship ever built. The Titanic!
jokes.setup: Building up the luxury and safety of their cruise ship
jokes.punchline: It's literally named the Titanic, the most famous doomed ship in history
jokes.explanation: Obvious but effective reference joke - the name drop is predictable but still works

jokes.text: Looks nice.
jokes.setup: Professor's dramatic announcement about the Titanic
jokes.punchline: Fry's completely oblivious, mundane reaction shows he missed the ominous implications
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat showing Fry's obliviousness, economical and well-placed

jokes.text: A man who single-handedly defeated the Retiree People... of the Assisted Living Nebula: Zapp Brannigan!
jokes.setup: Building up Zapp's military heroics
jokes.punchline: His great victory was against elderly people in assisted living, hardly a military achievement
jokes.explanation: Excellent world-building joke that establishes Zapp's character perfectly - pompous but incompetent

jokes.text: God, not Zapp Brannigan! / You know Zapp Brannigan? / Let's just say we've crossed paths.
jokes.setup: Leela's strong negative reaction to Zapp, Fry's innocent question
jokes.punchline: Leela's evasive 'crossed paths' euphemism hints at their romantic history
jokes.explanation: Good setup for their relationship, creates intrigue with understatement

jokes.text: Before or after you slept with him?
jokes.setup: Leela's evasive response about 'crossing paths' with Zapp
jokes.punchline: Amy immediately cuts through the euphemism with brutal directness
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and character work - Amy's bluntness cuts through Leela's attempt at discretion

jokes.text: She's a beautiful ship. Shapely, seductive. I'm gonna fly her brains out.
jokes.setup: Zapp describing the ship in romantic terms
jokes.punchline: The increasingly sexual language culminating in 'fly her brains out' double entendre
jokes.explanation: Solid character humor showing Zapp's sleazy personality through ship metaphor

jokes.text: And now, without further adieu... I christen this ship Titanic.
jokes.setup: Formal ship christening ceremony
jokes.punchline: Zapp says 'adieu' (goodbye) instead of 'ado' while naming it after a doomed ship
jokes.explanation: Clever malapropism that's ironically appropriate - saying 'goodbye' while christening the Titanic

jokes.text: Ooh!
jokes.setup: Zapp christening the ship
jokes.punchline: The crowd's reaction suggests something went wrong with the christening ceremony
jokes.explanation: Minor visual gag reaction, fairly standard crowd response

jokes.text: Well, well, well! The Lady Leela. Take notes, Kif. You're about to witness the subtle waltz known as seduction.
jokes.setup: Zapp announcing his intention to seduce Leela with 'subtle' technique
jokes.punchline: Nothing about Zapp is subtle - he's literally announcing his seduction attempt to his aide
jokes.explanation: Great character work - perfectly captures Zapp's oblivious narcissism and lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: So! Trapped on a ship with me. What say you and I knock some very sensual boots?
jokes.setup: Zapp's promised 'subtle seduction'
jokes.punchline: Immediately propositions her with crude directness, completely contradicting his claim of subtlety
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the 'subtle seduction' setup - shows Zapp learned nothing from past rejection

jokes.text: It's tempting, but I have a fiancé now.
jokes.setup: Zapp's crude proposition
jokes.punchline: Leela's sarcastic 'it's tempting' before revealing she's taken
jokes.explanation: Good character response showing Leela's dry wit and setting up the fake relationship plot

jokes.text: Huh? Ooh! / How do you do?
jokes.setup: Leela suddenly claiming Fry as her fiancé
jokes.punchline: Fry's confusion then awkward attempt to play along politely
jokes.explanation: Solid character reaction showing Fry's confusion but willingness to help

jokes.text: At least we get a window.
jokes.setup: Expectation of a terrible room
jokes.punchline: Fry's optimistic attempt to find a bright side in what's clearly a horrible accommodation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's eternal optimism even in bad circumstances

jokes.text: I'm tired of this room and everyone in it. Excuse me. I'm gonna pop over to the casino for the next 135 hours.
jokes.setup: Bender immediately getting tired of their shared room
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and long time period he plans to spend gambling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - selfish, addictive behavior with robot-like precision in the time estimate

jokes.text: I think I'll go fill up on bisque at the buffet. / That just leaves you and your fake fiancé... all alone on a romantic cruise.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg leaving for food
jokes.punchline: Amy pointing out the awkward romantic implications of the fake relationship
jokes.explanation: Good setup for romantic tension, creates appropriate awkwardness

jokes.text: I'm betting it all! Come on! Baby needs a new pair of feet!
jokes.setup: Classic gambling exclamation 'baby needs new shoes'
jokes.punchline: Bender's robot anatomy makes it 'feet' instead of shoes
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific twist on a classic phrase, perfectly executed

jokes.text: Snake eyes. / My cheating unit malfunctioned! You gotta give me a do-over.
jokes.setup: Bender losing at gambling
jokes.punchline: He openly admits to having a 'cheating unit' and acts like its malfunction entitles him to another chance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant robot humor - treats cheating as a normal mechanical function that can malfunction

jokes.text: Sorry, the house limit is three do-overs. Next shooter.
jokes.setup: Bender asking for a do-over for cheating malfunction
jokes.punchline: The casino apparently has an official policy for cheating malfunctions, and Bender has used his up
jokes.explanation: Great world-building humor that suggests this is a common enough occurrence to have rules

jokes.text: I assure you, I barely know the meaning of the word 'labor.'
jokes.setup: Countess commenting on robot class divisions
jokes.punchline: Bender's proud admission of laziness while trying to seem sophisticated
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line - his laziness becomes a point of pride when flirting

jokes.text: Kif, I'm feeling the captain's itch. / I'll get the powder.
jokes.setup: Zapp mentioning a 'captain's itch' - sounds medical
jokes.punchline: Kif immediately offering powder suggests it's a literal skin condition
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that subverts expectation of 'captain's itch' being metaphorical

jokes.text: The itch for adventure. Let's change course.
jokes.setup: Kif's literal interpretation of captain's itch
jokes.punchline: Zapp was actually being metaphorical after all, making Kif's response awkward
jokes.explanation: Good callback that makes Kif's literal interpretation even funnier in retrospect

jokes.text: That's for schoolgirls. Here's a route with chest hair.
jokes.setup: Zapp rejecting the safe planned route
jokes.punchline: Describing a route as having 'chest hair' - absurd masculinity applied to navigation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Zapp line showing his absurd machismo applied to completely inappropriate contexts

jokes.text: Yes, comets. The icebergs of the sky.
jokes.setup: Kif questioning the dangerous comet route
jokes.punchline: Zapp's pseudo-poetic but nonsensical comparison of comets to icebergs
jokes.explanation: Good character line showing Zapp's pretentious but empty way of speaking

jokes.text: It's time to shove a jalapeno up this ship's tailpipe.
jokes.setup: Zapp deciding to take risks with the ship
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual metaphor mixed with car terminology for a spaceship
jokes.explanation: Very Zapp-like crude metaphor that mixes sexual and automotive imagery inappropriately

jokes.text: We were enjoying a relaxing vacation. Since you're here, we must meddle.
jokes.setup: Amy's parents explaining their presence
jokes.punchline: Completely honest admission that they can't resist interfering in Amy's life
jokes.explanation: Perfect parent humor - brutally honest about their meddling nature

jokes.text: We met a nice boy in the next cabin. / He's not very ugly.
jokes.setup: Parents trying to set Amy up
jokes.punchline: Dad's backhanded compliment - 'not very ugly' as high praise
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing the parents' blunt, practical approach to romance

jokes.text: You should marry him. Or use him to conceive a grandchild for us.
jokes.setup: Parents trying to set Amy up with someone
jokes.punchline: Immediately jumping to marriage or just using him for reproduction
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing how practical and direct the parents are about reproduction

jokes.text: Where is he? And why isn't he here right now, fathering our grandchild?
jokes.setup: Amy mentioning she has a boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Dad immediately demanding to know why active reproduction isn't happening
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the parents' single-minded focus on grandchildren

jokes.text: It's romantic- I mean, platonic. That sure is one platonic view.
jokes.setup: Fry and Leela sharing a romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Fry's awkward attempt to correct himself makes it more obvious he has romantic feelings
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Fry's attempt to hide his feelings backfiring

jokes.text: I'm beginning to think this whole fake fiancé thing was a terrible- / Hm? Mmm.
jokes.setup: Leela about to express regret about the fake relationship
jokes.punchline: Fry kisses her mid-sentence, creating maximum awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Good romantic tension moment with perfect awkward timing

jokes.text: Perhaps this is an awkward time.
jokes.setup: Zapp walking in on Fry and Leela kissing
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of how awkward his interruption is
jokes.explanation: Good understatement that plays into the awkward romantic triangle

jokes.text: Well, you got anything else for him?
jokes.setup: Leela claiming the kiss was for Zapp's benefit
jokes.punchline: Fry's hopeful question suggesting more romantic gestures
jokes.explanation: Good Fry response showing his opportunistic nature with romantic possibilities

jokes.text: I consider my fake relationship with you more meaningful.
jokes.setup: Discussion of fake relationships
jokes.punchline: Leela's deadpan admission that even fake romance with Fry beats Amy's situation
jokes.explanation: Excellent line that's both touching and funny - fake relationship hierarchy humor

jokes.text: Either that was a computing error or you're the most romantic robot ever.
jokes.setup: Bender's overly precise romantic compliment
jokes.punchline: Countess acknowledging both the technical flaw and unexpected charm
jokes.explanation: Good response that plays into robot romance while acknowledging the absurdity

jokes.text: The bracelet had a diamond the size of a raccoon's brain. Why couldn't I steal it?
jokes.setup: Bender explaining his failed theft
jokes.punchline: Using a raccoon's brain as a unit of measurement for diamond size
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and absurd unit of measurement that's both funny and oddly precise

jokes.text: It's obvious. You're in love.
jokes.setup: Bender unable to steal from someone he's romancing
jokes.punchline: The ship's computer diagnosing his problem as love interfering with theft
jokes.explanation: Great robot logic - love as a malfunction preventing normal criminal behavior

jokes.text: She, a countess built with a silver spoon in her mouth... and me, just a regular honest Joe.
jokes.setup: Bender lamenting their class differences
jokes.punchline: Calling himself 'honest' when he just tried to rob her and everyone knows he's a criminal
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic self-description - Bender's complete lack of self-awareness about his dishonesty

jokes.text: You gonna pay for those? / Hell, no.
jokes.setup: Bartender asking if Bender will pay
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate honest refusal right after calling himself honest
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate contradiction of his 'honest Joe' claim - brilliant character consistency

jokes.text: Don't be a dog heart, woman. You know I can't. Not after what happened.
jokes.setup: Someone encouraging Hermes to limbo
jokes.punchline: His dramatic refusal hints at a traumatic limboing past
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Hermes's limbo backstory, creates intrigue and character depth

jokes.text: No, boy, stop! It's too low! Your backbone can't take it! / I'm just like Hermes! I'm just like- / No!
jokes.setup: Kid trying to imitate Hermes's limbo skills
jokes.punchline: The kid apparently breaks his back trying to limbo, explaining Hermes's trauma
jokes.explanation: Dark but brilliant backstory reveal - limbo competition causing spinal injury is absurd yet tragic

jokes.text: Though it did get me some action. / I like a man who's flexible.
jokes.setup: Discussion of limbo and masculinity
jokes.punchline: LaBarbara's comment about flexibility has clear sexual implications
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual innuendo but fits the characters and situation well

jokes.text: No matter. I know from personal experience how horizontal you can get.
jokes.setup: Zapp declaring Leela the limbo winner despite not participating
jokes.punchline: Clear sexual reference to their past encounter disguised as limbo talk
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp line that's both crude and cleverly disguised as innocent limbo commentary

jokes.text: I'm a fraud. A poor, lazy, sexy fraud.
jokes.setup: Bender confessing his deception to the Countess
jokes.punchline: Including 'sexy' in his list of negative traits shows his narcissism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - even in confession he can't help but compliment himself

jokes.text: This isn't even a real bow tie. It's magnetic.
jokes.setup: Bender revealing his fraudulent nature
jokes.punchline: The specific detail about the magnetic bow tie being somehow more dishonest
jokes.explanation: Good absurd detail that makes the confession more specific and robotic

jokes.text: I love your artificial intelligence and sincerity simulator.
jokes.setup: Bender's honest confession of deception
jokes.punchline: Countess loves him for his fake sincerity rather than despite it
jokes.explanation: Clever robot romance humor - love based on appreciation for simulated emotions

jokes.text: Not much younger. Perhaps even a couple years older.
jokes.setup: Zapp saying Fry reminds him of his younger self
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself to suggest Fry might be older
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp moment showing his vanity and inability to commit to compliments

jokes.text: I'll be all over Leela like a fly on a pile of very seductive manure.
jokes.setup: Zapp threatening to pursue Leela if Fry strays
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to a fly on 'seductive manure' - disgusting metaphor meant to be romantic
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly awful Zapp metaphor - manages to be both disgusting and romantic in his mind

jokes.text: I learned how to handle delicate situations... from a show called Three's Company.
jokes.setup: Fry facing a complicated romantic situation
jokes.punchline: His solution is to copy a famously farcical sitcom known for misunderstandings
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke that predicts the farce to come while showing Fry's pop culture solutions

jokes.text: Come and knock on our door
jokes.setup: Fry invoking Three's Company as his solution
jokes.punchline: He literally starts singing the theme song to stall for time
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Fry's desperation by literally invoking the theme song

jokes.text: Mr. Roper....
jokes.setup: Fry continuing to invoke Three's Company
jokes.punchline: He's treating Zapp like Mr. Roper, the confused authority figure from the show
jokes.explanation: Continues the Three's Company bit but less effectively than the setup

jokes.text: Come back when it's a catastrophe.
jokes.setup: Kif trying to report an emergency
jokes.punchline: Zapp's dismissive escalation from emergency to catastrophe shows poor priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp line showing terrible leadership priorities - only responds to maximum danger

jokes.text: I don't know which disgusts me more, your cowardice or stupidity.
jokes.setup: Kif reporting that their course is proving suicidal
jokes.punchline: Zapp's response treats survival instincts as character flaws
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp characterization showing his toxic masculinity and poor judgment

jokes.text: We'll set a new course for that region... near that black-ish hole-ish thing.
jokes.setup: Zapp deciding to change course from the dangerous route
jokes.punchline: His 'safer' alternative is heading toward a black hole
jokes.explanation: Great Zapp moment - his course correction is even more dangerous than the original

jokes.text: Are you sure? I mean, she has two eyes. You have two eyes.
jokes.setup: Fry claiming he's not interested in Amy
jokes.punchline: Leela's insecurity about her one eye expressed through 'logical' compatibility
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Leela's insecurity about her unique feature affecting romance

jokes.text: Talk about a mood killer.
jokes.setup: The ship being pulled into a black hole during a romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Fry's massive understatement of their life-threatening situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement humor - treating mortal peril as mere romantic inconvenience

jokes.text: That's surprisingly noble of you. / No, it's noble of you.
jokes.setup: Zapp claiming he'll go down with the ship
jokes.punchline: He immediately makes Leela the new captain, avoiding the noble sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Zapp moment - perfectly executed cowardly escape disguised as honor

jokes.text: Don't tell the others. If I don't return, say I died robbing some old man.
jokes.setup: Bender going back to save the Countess in a heroic moment
jokes.punchline: He wants to be remembered for a crime rather than heroism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Bender's heroism compromised by wanting a criminal legacy

jokes.text: I'll say you died prying the ring off his dead finger.
jokes.setup: Bender wanting to be remembered for robbing an old man
jokes.punchline: Fry escalates it to grave robbing, making it even worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Fry understanding and supporting Bender's criminal image

jokes.text: No, luckily a family broke my fall.
jokes.setup: Bender asking if she's hurt from falling through the deck
jokes.punchline: Casual admission that she landed on people, likely injuring them
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect casualness - treating human cushions as fortunate

jokes.text: And I'd do it again! And perhaps a third time! But that would be it.
jokes.setup: Bender declaring his love after heroic rescue
jokes.punchline: Limiting his heroic gestures to a maximum of three times total
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - romantic declaration with built-in limits on future heroism

jokes.text: When we kiss, I feel like I'm standing waist-deep... in a pool of cold, rising water.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment calling for poetic description
jokes.punchline: Bender's 'romantic' metaphor is actually describing drowning/disaster
jokes.explanation: Brilliant robot romance - what should be poetic becomes ominous and mechanical

jokes.text: This one is for that little kid limboing in heaven now.
jokes.setup: Hermes preparing for heroic limbo to save everyone
jokes.punchline: Dedication to the kid who died trying to copy his limbo moves
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that turns dark backstory into heroic motivation

jokes.text: We needn't dress up like women and children.
jokes.setup: Zapp noting there are plenty of escape pods
jokes.punchline: Implies he was prepared to dress in drag to get priority escape seating
jokes.explanation: Good Zapp cowardice humor - reveals his backup plans for self-preservation

jokes.text: More importantly, we found you a man. / A captain.
jokes.setup: Amy's parents survived disaster
jokes.punchline: Their priority is still matchmaking, now upgrading to captain rank
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - even in disaster they're focused on Amy's love life

jokes.text: After all, it's shareware.
jokes.setup: Countess telling Bender he'll find love again
jokes.punchline: Her final words are a computer pun about sharing love like software
jokes.explanation: Brilliant final line mixing genuine emotion with robot-specific humor

jokes.text: Tragic romances always have a happy ending.
jokes.setup: Countess about to sacrifice herself
jokes.punchline: Bender completely misunderstands how tragic romances work
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Bender's optimism is exactly wrong about tragic romance genre

jokes.text: Don't cry. Nobody knows what happens in a black hole. It's possible she's alive in another dimension, right? / Oh, yes. Absolutely. / Not a chance.
jokes.setup: Amy trying to comfort Bender with scientific possibility
jokes.punchline: Professor's false reassurance immediately contradicted by Zoidberg's honesty
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - Professor's social lie immediately undermined by Zoidberg's bluntness

jokes.text: It's fake, man.
jokes.setup: Bender asking about the value of the Countess's bracelet
jokes.punchline: The romantic symbol of their relationship was worthless all along
jokes.explanation: Perfect bitter ending - the symbol of love was as fake as everything else, delivered with economy
